description: Candidate documentation frameworks for a public REST API docs site. Capability flags are normalized from each framework's official documentation. For each capability a value is one of: 'builtin' (works out of the box / first-party), 'plugin' (supported only via an additional plugin/integration you must install+configure), or 'no' (not supported). The requirement is a docs site with: syntax-highlighted code blocks, light/dark mode, full-text search, doc VERSIONING (so /v1 and /v2 can coexist), and rendering an OpenAPI/Swagger spec as interactive API reference.

evidence.openapi_rendering: Via the docusaurus-openapi-docs plugin (github.com/PaloAltoNetworks/docusaurus-openapi-docs) — not core.
evidence.search: Algolia DocSearch or local-search plugin; not core.
evidence.code_blocks: Built-in Prism code blocks with tabs.

candidates.popularity_note: very widely used for product/API docs

evidence.versioning: Via the external 'mike' tool (setup/setting-up-versioning) — not core.
evidence.openapi_rendering: Via third-party plugins (neoteroi-mkdocs / render_swagger) — not core.
Purpose-built OpenAPI reference renderer (its core function).
evidence.search: Built-in reference search within the spec.
evidence.versioning: Renders ONE spec; no multi-version docs site versioning.
evidence.code_blocks: Not a general docs/markdown site — no authored guide pages with code blocks.
evidence.dark_mode: Theme customization only via config/paid Redocly; no simple toggle in OSS.

candidates.popularity_note: the standard OpenAPI reference renderer, but NOT a full docs site

evidence.versioning: No first-party multi-version docs system (community workarounds only).
No first-party versioning (community plugins like starlight-versions).
Read the Docs hosting provides first-party multi-version docs.
evidence.dark_mode: Classic RTD theme has no built-in dark-mode toggle (needs a different theme).

candidates.popularity_note: classic for software docs, esp. Python
